comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 82, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order, no numbered prefixes):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL 15 WIs — do NOT summarize, do NOT group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new test file exactly as specified in deliverables) D. FILES TO MODIFY (list every existing test file you will add tests to) E. FILES TO READ (all production source files you will read for context) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name, every test file with its test class/function names and naming conventions, ledger path + frontmatter) G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite VERBATIM the exact git diff --name-only grep command and the expected EMPTY output) H. MATURE-NOT-MINIMAL 1-HOUR CAP ACKNOWLEDGMENT (one sentence) I. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (list any; at minimum address: asyncio.to_thread() verification strategy without a real Textual App loop, PA handshake backoff monkeypatching strategy, constants-pinning vs shared/constants re-export chain, boot-poll attempt-marker determinism without real timers) J. PRODUCTION FILE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote the prohibition verbatim)
After posting, STOP. Do NOT proceed to implementation. You cannot begin code work until you have posted your [agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension] comment AND received [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED from the SDO.
If SDO issues VERDICT: ADJUST, update your comprehension gate and wait again.

milestone.objective: Close all test-coverage, boundary-verification, and assertion-quality gaps in the UI Gateway (`services/ui_gateway/`) and UI Shell (`services/ui_shell/`) service clusters scoped by `docs/P5_TASK8_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml` §5 (EA-3 block, lines 255-274) and `docs/sprints/sprint_8/strategic_design_vision.md` §5.1 item 3. Each WI below cites its authoritative production-file line range directly; scope is prompt-internal (no external audit artifact). This EA addresses: the STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT overflow exact-boundary guard in transport.py (lines 545-550); the `stream_tokens()` malformed-message / decode-error continue path (transport.py:534-540); the `check_pa_status()` already-connected short-circuit (transport.py:258-260); the PA handshake retry / backoff sequence (transport.py:265-291); the `action_submit_prompt()` four branches — PGOV-denied, PGOV-approved, RuntimeError, generic Exception (app.py:405-445); the boot-poll attempt-marker progression (app.py:237-284); `SessionPanel` public method surface plus the `asyncio.to_thread()` wiring (session_panel.py full file); the `SessionListItem` label text format (session_panel.py:45-48); streaming `_streaming` flag transitions (streaming.py full file); the tool-call buffer boundary (transport.py:648); and direct constants-pinning tests for both UI Gateway and UI Shell constants. No production code is modified. All work is pure test authoring.
milestone.ledger_frontmatter: --- ledger_id: <YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint8_ea3_ui-hardening date: <YYYY-MM-DD> sprint_id: 8 entry_type: EA predecessor: 20260422_184004_sprint8_ea2_ao_sr_hardening branch: feature/p5-task8-ea3-ui-hardening merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
milestone.depends_on: EA-2 merged to main. Branch feature/p5-task8-ea2-ao-sr-hardening must be present in main history (verified at authoring: merge commit 0b5e5ec, Co-Lead completion report df686b8). If main has advanced further, use the current main HEAD.

item.title: Add STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT exact-boundary overflow test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (MODIFY) Class: TestStreamTokensBufferLimit (CREATE NEW CLASS)
Tests that pin overflow-guard behavior of `stream_tokens()` at the exact STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT boundary. Read the actual value from services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py at pickup; do NOT hardcode.
test_stream_tokens_at_limit_yields_last_token: Drive the stream with exactly STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT tokens. Assert all LIMIT tokens are yielded in order; stream terminates cleanly without overflow log.
test_stream_tokens_one_over_limit_breaks: Drive with STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT + 1 tokens. Assert exactly LIMIT tokens are yielded; stream breaks on the (LIMIT+1)th; Fail-Closed error log is emitted.
Read before writing: services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (stream_tokens method; locate the overflow guard and surrounding yield/break structure) services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py (STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT value) services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (existing stream_tokens test patterns; mirror the mocking style)
Mocking: reuse the existing async generator / mock connection seam. Do not add new production hooks.

item.title: Add stream_tokens malformed-message / decode-error continue-path test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (MODIFY) Class: TestStreamTokensDecodeError (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_stream_tokens_malformed_frame_skips_and_continues: Inject a frame sequence: [valid_token_0, malformed_payload, valid_token_1, final_token]. Malformed decode must raise the production-realistic exception (`ValueError` or `json.JSONDecodeError` — read transport.py to determine which — use that exact type in the mock). Assert: generator yields valid_token_0 and valid_token_1 (and final payload). Malformed frame produces logger.error but does NOT raise or terminate.
If transport.py catches `ValueError`, inject raising ValueError. If it catches `json.JSONDecodeError`, raise JSONDecodeError. If broader catch, cover both. Test asserts CURRENT production behavior, not an assumed one.
Read before writing: services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (stream_tokens decode catch path) services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (frame-mocking patterns)

item.title: Add check_pa_status already-connected short-circuit test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (MODIFY) Class: TestCheckPaStatusShortCircuit (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_check_pa_status_returns_true_when_already_connected: Construct the gateway. Pre-set its internal _connected flag (or whatever transport.py uses as its already-connected sentinel — read to determine). Call await gateway.check_pa_status(). Assert: returns True. Assert: no handshake/send path was invoked (Mock on the framer send or socket send; assert_not_called()).
Read before writing: services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (check_pa_status and its `if self._connected: ... return True` branch; identify the real attribute name and the send-path method to assert not-called) services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (gateway construction pattern)

item.title: Add action_submit_prompt PGOV-denied branch test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (MODIFY — add NEW tests; do NOT rewrite existing tests) Class: TestActionSubmitPromptBranches (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_action_submit_prompt_pgov_denied_displays_panel_and_flushes_rejected: Mock gateway.send_prompt() (or equivalent) to return a result with `approved=False` and a denial reason code from PGOV_REASON_LABELS (read services/ui_shell/src/constants.py for the authoritative label set). Invoke the BlarAIApp's action_submit_prompt flow. Construction-only strategy (see RISK I.1): construct the App, stub widget lookups with Mocks, call the method directly. Assert: PGOV panel display_denial called exactly once with the result. Assert: tool-call buffer flush invoked with pgov_approved=False (or whichever argument the production path uses — read app.py). Assert: no approved-tokens persistence occurred.
If the Textual App test seam truly cannot be established without a live loop, STOP and raise a blocker — do NOT add a production seam.

item.title: Add action_submit_prompt PGOV-approved branch test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (MODIFY) Class: TestActionSubmitPromptBranches (same class as WI-4)
test_action_submit_prompt_pgov_approved_persists_and_streams: Mock gateway.send_prompt() to return a result with `approved=True` and a non-empty approved-token stream. Invoke action_submit_prompt per WI-4's construction strategy. Assert: approved-token flush invoked with pgov_approved=True. Assert: tokens fed to the streaming widget (or its mock). Assert: PGOV panel display_denial NOT called.

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-3 + services/ui_shell/src/app.py:438-445 (RuntimeError and Exception handlers)
item.title: Add action_submit_prompt RuntimeError and generic-Exception handler tests
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (MODIFY) Class: TestActionSubmitPromptBranches (same class as WI-4/5)
test_action_submit_prompt_runtime_error_displays_specific_message: Mock gateway.send_prompt() to raise RuntimeError("boom"). Invoke action_submit_prompt. Assert: display_output_line (or equivalent — read app.py) called with a message containing "boom" and the production RuntimeError styling (e.g., "[red]Error:…"). Assert: no unexpected-error / fail-closed message was written.
test_action_submit_prompt_generic_exception_displays_fail_closed: Mock gateway.send_prompt() to raise Exception("unknown"). Invoke action_submit_prompt. Assert: display output line called with exact fail-closed message defined in app.py. Assert: error logged via logger.exception or logger.error — read app.py.
Use caplog for log assertions. Read exact log-level and message text from app.py; do NOT invent strings.

item.title: Strengthen tautological / stub assertions in test_app.py
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (MODIFY)
Review existing `test_submit_noop_when_not_operational` and `test_submit_noop_when_no_gateway` (approx lines 74-86). They check preconditions without actually invoking action_submit_prompt. Either:
(a) Strengthen them: invoke action_submit_prompt under each precondition and assert gateway.send_prompt (mock) was NOT called — i.e. the guard short-circuits the action.
OR, if reaching the action method requires production seam changes:
(b) Leave stubs intact but add new tests in TestActionSubmitPromptBranches (WI-4/5/6) that explicitly call action_submit_prompt under each guard condition and assert the send-path was not invoked.
Preferred: (a). Use (b) only if (a) would require a production change.
Read: services/ui_shell/src/app.py (action_submit_prompt top-of-method guards) services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (lines 74-86; identify guard shape)

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-3 + SDV §5.1 item 3 + services/ui_shell/src/session_panel.py (full file)
item.title: Create dedicated test_session_panel.py covering public methods + to_thread wiring
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_session_panel.py (CREATE NEW) Classes: TestSessionPanelPublicMethods, TestSessionListItemLabelFormat
Public methods (async): refresh_list, create_new_session, delete_current_session, select_session. Property: active_session_id.
For each async method: Construct SessionPanel with a Mock session_store. Use pytest-asyncio (confirm existing pattern). Call the method. Assert store methods were invoked via asyncio.to_thread — the synchronous store method was called exactly once with correct arguments. Monkeypatch `asyncio.to_thread` to a thin wrapper that records invocations (see RISK I.2).
The point: the UI Shell must never call store methods synchronously from the async context. Test must fail if a future refactor replaces `await asyncio.to_thread(store.x)` with `store.x()`.
test_session_list_item_label_format: Construct SessionSummary with known title and turn_count. Instantiate SessionListItem; render its label. Assert label text matches production format exactly. Read session_panel.py for the format string.
Read: services/ui_shell/src/session_panel.py (full file) services/ui_shell/src/constants.py (TITLE_PLACEHOLDER) services/ui_gateway/src/session_store.py (SessionSummary shape) services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py, test_streaming.py (async patterns)

item.title: Add boot-poll attempt-marker progression test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_app.py (MODIFY) Class: TestBootPollAttemptMarkers (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_poll_boot_status_advances_all_attempt_markers: Exercise the boot-poll loop that displays "attempt N/PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES" banner updates. Use a controlled time source (monkeypatch time.monotonic or equivalent) and a mocked gateway whose check_pa_status returns False for the first two attempt windows and True on the third. Assert: exactly PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES attempt-banner updates were emitted in order (attempt 1/3, 2/3, 3/3). Assert: final status transitions to "connected" when check_pa_status returns True.
If the loop is too entangled with Textual widget setup, cover only the attempt-marker LIST computation (app.py:237-242) as a pure unit test. Document partial coverage in the completion report.
Read: services/ui_shell/src/app.py (attempt_markers computation; poll loop) services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py (PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES, PA_HANDSHAKE_BACKOFF_BASE_S)

item.title: Tighten PA handshake retry / backoff assertions
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (MODIFY) Class: TestPaHandshakeRetry (CREATE or extend existing)
Audit existing check_pa_status tests: - Assert the exact number of asyncio.sleep calls? - Assert exact backoff durations (1.0s, 2.0s)? - Assert sleep is called BEFORE retries 2/3 but NOT after the final? If any are missing, add them.
test_check_pa_status_backoff_sequence: Mock handshake to fail twice then succeed on attempt 3. Monkeypatch asyncio.sleep to record arguments (do NOT sleep — AsyncMock returns immediately — see RISK I.8). Assert: recorded sleep durations are [PA_HANDSHAKE_BACKOFF_BASE_S, PA_HANDSHAKE_BACKOFF_BASE_S * 2] (i.e. [1.0, 2.0] at base 1.0). No sleep after the successful third attempt.
test_check_pa_status_exhausts_retries_returns_false: Mock handshake to fail all PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES attempts. Assert: returns False. Assert: sleep was called exactly (MAX_RETRIES - 1) times.
Read: services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (check_pa_status retry loop) services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py (PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES, PA_HANDSHAKE_BACKOFF_BASE_S)

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-3 + services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py (full file — _streaming flag transitions)
item.title: Add streaming._streaming state-transition tests
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_streaming.py (MODIFY) Class: TestStreamingFlagTransitions (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_streaming_flag_true_during_non_final_tokens: Construct the StreamingWidget (or StreamingBuffer — read streaming.py for the correct class name). Call append_token() with is_final=False five times. Assert: is_streaming == True throughout and after.
test_streaming_flag_false_after_final_token: Continue the above with is_final=True. Assert: is_streaming flips to False.
test_streaming_flag_false_after_clear_display: Enter streaming state. Call clear_display() (or equivalent). Assert: _streaming is False and any partial buffer was released.
test_streaming_flag_false_after_start_new_response: Enter streaming state. Call start_new_response(). Assert: _streaming is False (or whatever the production path does — read streaming.py).
Read: services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py (full file — identify real method names; may differ from generic names above) services/ui_shell/tests/test_streaming.py (existing patterns)

item.title: Create test_constants_ui_gateway.py constants-pinning tests
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_constants_ui_gateway.py (CREATE NEW) Class: TestUiGatewayConstants
Direct-assertion tests pinning every constant in services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py. Enumerate by reading constants.py at pickup (do NOT trust the audit snapshot — source is authoritative).
Known constants (verify at pickup): SESSION_DB_DIR (see RISK I.7 — path-shape assertion) SESSION_DB_FILENAME == "sessions.db" SESSION_DB_PATH (derived; assert endswith(SESSION_DB_FILENAME)) PA_HANDSHAKE_MAX_RETRIES == 3 PA_HANDSHAKE_BACKOFF_BASE_S == 1.0 PA_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_S == 5.0 PROMPT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_S == 120.0 STREAM_TOKEN_BUFFER_LIMIT == 4096 TOOL_CALL_BUFFER_MAX_TOKENS == 512 SESSION_TITLE_MAX_CHARS == 80
If any constant is a re-export from shared/constants.py (VSOCK_PORT, ORCHESTRATOR_VM_ID, etc.), pin the re-export: assert `ui_gateway.constants.X is shared_ref` (identity via `is`, not ==). See services/semantic_router/tests/test_constants_sr.py for the idiom.
Read: services/ui_gateway/src/constants.py shared/constants.py (for re-exports) services/semantic_router/tests/test_constants_sr.py (re-export idiom)

item.title: Create test_constants_ui_shell.py constants-pinning tests
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_constants_ui_shell.py (CREATE NEW) Class: TestUiShellConstants
Same approach as WI-12 for services/ui_shell/src/constants.py.
Known constants (verify at pickup): SESSION_PANEL_WIDTH_PCT == 25 PROMPT_MAX_CHARS == 4096 RESPONSE_SCROLL_BACK_LINES == 10000 TITLE_PLACEHOLDER == "New session" KEY_SUBMIT == "enter", KEY_RETRY == "ctrl+r", KEY_NEW_SESSION == "ctrl+n", KEY_DELETE_SESSION == "ctrl+d", KEY_QUIT == "ctrl+q" STREAM_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS == 50 CURSOR_BLINK_INTERVAL_MS == 500 PGOV_PANEL_BORDER_STYLE == "heavy" PGOV_DENIAL_TITLE == "Policy Denial" PGOV_REASON_LABELS (dict — assert exact 6 keys and exact labels) BOOT_STATUS_POLL_INTERVAL_S == 1.0 BOOT_BANNER_TEXT (exact string) BOOT_FAILED_TEXT (exact string)
For PGOV_REASON_LABELS, enumerate every key-value pair in one test (`test_pgov_reason_labels_complete_mapping`) so any rename/edit fails loudly.
Read: services/ui_shell/src/constants.py services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_constants_ao.py (idiom)

item.title: Add tool_call_buffer exact-boundary overflow test
item.detail:
File: services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (MODIFY) Class: TestToolCallBufferBoundary (CREATE NEW CLASS)
test_tool_call_buffer_at_limit_minus_one_accepts_next_token: Fill the buffer with TOOL_CALL_BUFFER_MAX_TOKENS - 1 tokens. Append one more. Assert no exception (buffer now at exactly MAX_TOKENS). Append one more. Assert raises ValueError with the production error message.
Read: services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (tool_call buffer append path) services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py (existing overflow test)

item.title: Scan UI tests for deprecated loop.run_until_complete() and migrate
item.detail:
Files: services/ui_gateway/tests/**, services/ui_shell/tests/** (SCAN; MODIFY any matches)
Grep `run_until_complete` across both test trees. Per the scope audit, no instances were found — re-verify at pickup. If any surface, migrate to pytest-asyncio `@pytest.mark.asyncio` + `await`.
If zero instances are found, WI-15 closes with a single-line entry in the completion report confirming the scan result. No code changes required.

constraint.detail: Zero production source files may be modified. Specifically: NO edits to services/ui_gateway/src/**, services/ui_shell/src/**, shared/**, launcher/**, or services/*/src/**. If a production change appears necessary to unblock a test (e.g., to add a seam), STOP, document the blocker in the completion report, and wait for SDO direction.
Do not rename existing test files or move them. EA-5 owns structural cleanup. New test files live in services/ui_gateway/tests/ or services/ui_shell/tests/ as specified per WI.

Do NOT append to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (frozen at Entry 52). Write a NEW per-file entry at docs/ledger/<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS> _sprint8_ea3_ui-hardening.md with the frontmatter block from <ledger_frontmatter>. Timestamp in UTC at second resolution. Predecessor: 20260422_184004_sprint8_ea2_ao_sr_hardening.
pyproject.toml edits permitted ONLY for marker adjustments. No new packages. All tests run under existing pytest-asyncio and Mock/AsyncMock stack.

constraint.rule: No real sockets, no real vsock
constraint.detail: All transport tests MUST mock the underlying connection / framer / socket. Do NOT attempt a real Hyper-V VM, real vsock endpoint, or real Policy Agent. Mirror existing test_transport.py mock patterns.
Do NOT start the Textual App event loop. UI shell app.py tests construct the App class, stub widget lookups with Mocks, exercise method-level behavior only. See RISK I.1.
If a test requires a hook not already present in production, STOP. Do NOT add `_for_testing` attributes, test-only branches, or public setters. Report the blocker.

Write ONLY to services/ui_gateway/tests/ and services/ui_shell/tests/. Do not touch services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/, services/policy_agent/ tests/, services/semantic_router/tests/, shared/tests/, launcher/tests/, or tests/integration/.

risk.topic: Textual App testing without a live loop
risk.resolution: Construction-only strategy: `app = BlarAIApp(...); app._gateway = Mock(); app._pgov_panel = Mock(); await app.action_submit_prompt()`. Stub every widget lookup action_submit_prompt performs by pre-setting attributes to Mocks (AsyncMock for awaited calls). Do NOT call `app.run()` or `app.on_mount()`. If action_submit_prompt performs `self.query_one(...)` that cannot be stubbed via attribute assignment, patch `BlarAIApp.query_one` on the instance to return a Mock. If the method requires a live DOM, STOP and raise the blocker.

Use pytest-asyncio. Monkeypatch `asyncio.to_thread` with a thin async wrapper that records the call and invokes the synchronous target directly: recorded_calls = [] async def fake_to_thread(fn, *args, **kwargs): recorded_calls.append((fn, args, kwargs)) return fn(*args, **kwargs) monkeypatch.setattr(asyncio, 'to_thread', fake_to_thread) After exercising the SessionPanel method, assert recorded_calls contains the expected store method + args. Do NOT use a real thread.

Python 3.12+ warnings about `asyncio.get_event_loop()` when no loop is running are out of scope. WI-15 addresses only `run_until_complete()` migration. If a new test needs an event loop, use `@pytest.mark.asyncio` or `asyncio.run(...)` at test level. Do NOT introduce `get_event_loop()` in new tests.

risk.topic: EA-2 overlap — test_pgov_display.py line 121 was fixed in EA-2
risk.resolution: Per Sprint 8 EA-2 ledger (20260422_184004_sprint8_ea2_ao_sr_hardening.md, WI-10), the line-121 assertion bug in test_pgov_display.py is fixed. EA-3 does NOT re-touch this file unless a sibling assignment-posing-as-assertion bug surfaces during scan. If found, fix it as adjacent work per the mature-not-minimal clause.

risk.topic: Production string authority — error messages, banners, label formats
risk.resolution: Production source is ALWAYS authoritative for exact string values (error messages, banner text, PGOV_REASON_LABELS entries, label format strings). Read source at pickup; do NOT copy from this prompt. If audit-reported value differs from source, trust source and record the discrepancy in the completion report.

risk.topic: Boot-poll loop entanglement with Textual widgets
risk.resolution: If the boot-poll loop (app.py:237-284) cannot be driven to completion without a live App, WI-9 falls back to pure unit coverage of the `attempt_markers` list computation (lines 237-242). Document as partial coverage in the completion report. Do NOT add a production seam.

risk.topic: LOCALAPPDATA-dependent SESSION_DB_DIR on non-Windows test runs
risk.resolution: SESSION_DB_DIR is constructed from os.environ["LOCALAPPDATA"]. On CI/test the env var is present. For WI-12, test asserts: - SESSION_DB_DIR.endswith("BlarAI") (or the actual subdir — read source) - os.path.isabs(SESSION_DB_DIR) is True Do NOT assert exact absolute path (user-specific). Do NOT monkeypatch LOCALAPPDATA — constants.py evaluated at import time uses whatever was in the environment at import.

risk.topic: asyncio.sleep monkeypatching without hanging the test
risk.resolution: Replace `asyncio.sleep` with an AsyncMock that returns immediately: recorded_sleeps = [] async def fake_sleep(duration): recorded_sleeps.append(duration) monkeypatch.setattr(asyncio, 'sleep', fake_sleep) After the exercised code completes, assert on recorded_sleeps. Runs instantly and deterministically.

quality_gate.gate: Import smoke from repo root: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_gateway.tests.test_transport" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_shell.tests.test_app" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_shell.tests.test_session_panel" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_shell.tests.test_streaming" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_gateway.tests.test_constants_ui_gateway" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.ui_shell.tests.test_constants_ui_shell" No ImportError/ModuleNotFoundError is acceptable.

Run the UI slice: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest services/ui_gateway/ services/ui_shell/ --tb=short -q Expected: all new WI-1..WI-15 tests pass; pre-existing tests do not regress. Count delta ≥ 14 new tests.

Full regression: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q PASS criteria: post-EA-3 passed count ≥ post-EA-2 count (861 passed, 2 skipped per EA-2 ledger). Floor: 861 + 14 = 875. Record both numbers in the commit message. No pre-existing tests may regress.

Verify no production files were modified: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea3-ui-hardening --name-only | grep -vE "tests|conftest|docs|pyproject" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines). Any production file in the filtered diff is an L-15 mandate violation. STOP and report.

verification.description: Verbatim terminal commands the Lead Architect (non-developer) can run to confirm success. Run from C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI in PowerShell.

step.expected: Working directory set to the BlarAI repo root.

Most recent commit on main should be the EA-3 merge commit. Subject begins: test(task82/ea3): UI Gateway + UI Shell boundary + branch + constants hardening — followed by "N new tests, baseline X → Y".

step.command: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea3-ui-hardening --name-only | findstr /v /R "tests conftest docs pyproject"

step.expected: All ui_gateway and ui_shell tests pass, including new WI-1..WI-15 tests.
At least (post-EA-2 baseline, 861 passed, 2 skipped) + 14 = 875 passed, 2 skipped. Exact counts in the EA-3 commit message.

commit_template.format: test(task82/ea3): UI Gateway + UI Shell boundary + branch + constants hardening — {N} new tests, baseline {X} → {Y}
commit_template.example: test(task82/ea3): UI Gateway + UI Shell boundary + branch + constants hardening — 22 new tests, baseline 861 → 883
commit_template.rules: - N = exact count of new tests (verify with `pytest --co -q` diff). - X = total passed on main BEFORE the branch. - Y = total passed AFTER merge. - Commit ONLY files under services/ui_gateway/tests/, services/ui_shell/tests/, and docs/ledger/. - Do NOT commit production source files.

mature_not_minimal.directive:
EA-3 is authorized to address directly adjacent findings discovered during work — even if not pre-listed in the scope audit — provided each new item adds ≤ 1 hour (per SDV §5.3).
Examples of in-scope adjacent work: - While adding WI-8 SessionPanel tests, you notice a related SessionListItem compose() edge case (empty title, None turn_count). Add coverage if ≤ 1h. - While reading transport.py for WI-1, you discover a third overflow site missed by the audit. Add coverage. - While fixing stub tests in WI-7, you find a sibling test with the same stub idiom. Fix it too. - While pinning constants in WI-12/13, you notice a constant used in production but untested. Add coverage.
Items EXCEEDING the 1-hour cap: - Document in the completion report with estimated effort. - Co-Lead opens a next-sprint Vikunja task. - Do NOT absorb silently.
EA-3 addresses UI Gateway and UI Shell ONLY. AO/SR are EA-2 scope (merged); shared/launcher/integration are EA-4 scope; structural cleanup is EA-5 scope.
